museum.name: Gaming Time Machine
museum.dek: May 2010: Red Dead Redemption and Alan Wake make May unusually atmospheric.
museum.curatorNote: Initial researched draft for May 2010. Regional timing and UK retail context should be checked before verification.

sections.title: News
sections.subtitle: Five researched month markers or context notes.

items.title: Red Dead Redemption and Alan Wake make May unusually atmospheric.
items.body: A period-context note for the month, written cautiously where the evidence is broader than one exact day.

items.body: A period-context note for the month, written cautiously where the evidence is broader than one exact day.

sections.title: Releases
sections.subtitle: Eight notable releases, led by month-specific anchors where evidence supports them.

items.title: Alan Wake
items.body: Remedy turns episodic TV, torches and manuscript pages into a cult mood.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for May 2010.

items.title: Red Dead Redemption
items.body: Rockstar's western turns emptiness, travel and endings into blockbuster language.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for May 2010.

items.title: Mass Effect 2
items.body: BioWare turns the squad loyalty mission into a generation-defining rhythm.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for January 2010.

items.title: BioShock 2
items.body: Rapture returns as franchise space rather than one-off miracle.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for February 2010.

items.title: Battlefield: Bad Company 2
items.body: Destruction and squad chatter make the shooter feel muddy and loud.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for March 2010.

items.title: God of War III
items.body: Kratos gives PS3 a huge mythological showpiece.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for March 2010.

items.title: Limbo
items.body: A monochrome downloadable game becomes a quiet prestige object.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for July 2010.

items.title: Halo: Reach
items.body: Bungie says farewell to Halo with a doomed military elegy.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for September 2010.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.subtitle: Platform, buying and industry context for the month and its wider year.

items.title: PlayStation Move launches
items.body: Sony answers Wii with a glowing wand, camera tracking and a more precise motion-control pitch.
items.sourceNote: PlayStation Blog dates Move's worldwide launch from September 2010.

items.title: Kinect launches
items.body: Microsoft removes the controller entirely and sells the body as interface.
items.sourceNote: Xbox Wire dates Kinect for November 2010.

items.title: Nintendo 3DS is announced
items.body: Nintendo points beyond DS with glasses-free stereoscopic 3D.
items.sourceNote: 2010 hardware references support the March 2010 announcement context.

items.title: Download stores mature
items.body: XBLA, PSN, Steam and mobile stores make smaller games part of routine browsing.
items.sourceNote: Year-level platform context; verify individual store milestones before final copy.

sections.title: Online Life
sections.subtitle: How the network felt around the edges of play.

items.title: Services and servers
items.body: Online life is Xbox Live parties, PSN friends, Steam sales, YouTube trailers, forums and mobile games quietly filling spare minutes.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Downloads and stores
items.body: Minecraft spreads through videos, forums and word of mouth rather than traditional retail.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Forums and feeds
items.body: DLC and map packs feel normal enough to be complained about as normal things.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Everyday connection
items.body: XBLA and PSN make the console dashboard feel like a shop, not just a launcher.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

sections.title: What It Felt Like
sections.subtitle: A curator's narrative sketch of the month as lived culture.

items.title: The room
items.body: It felt like the living room had become a testing ground. Wands, cameras, balance boards and controllers all competed for hands and bodies.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The shelf
items.body: At the same time, some of the year's most lasting futures were quiet: Minecraft builds, Limbo silhouettes, Steam habits.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The conversation
items.body: The boxed blockbuster still ruled the shop wall, but the dashboard was starting to matter just as much.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The afterimage
items.body: For UK players, 2010 was motion-control adverts, Red Dead sunsets, Halo nights and the strange feeling that small downloads could be important.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.
